scenario: Target domain (ECI) is unreachable or timing out
handling_logic: The scraper must catch the error, log it as a warning, and immediately pivot to the internal FALLBACK_CONTEXT library to provide the user with high-fidelity, pre-verified electoral protocols.
expected_result: System surfaces official protocol text from fallback instead of an empty result.

scenario: User enters a malformed or malicious URL in the Verify field
handling_logic: The parseArticleUrl function filters URLs to only include 'http' starts and uses a strict DOM-only text extraction (stripping <script> and <style> tags) to prevent XSS or code execution.
expected_result: The malformed URL is ignored or safely parsed as plain text without executing code.

scenario: Query is ambiguous or has no available news coverage
handling_logic: The Gemini prompt is instructed to 'USE YOUR OWN KNOWLEDGE' if context is insufficient, ensuring the user always receives a substantive analysis instead of a 'No info' response.
expected_result: AI provides a general electoral context based on its pre-trained high-fidelity data.

scenario: Multiple rapid-fire requests from the same IP
handling_logic: An in-memory rate limiter (rateLimitMap) intercepts the request and returns an HTTP 429 status code with a helpful 'Please wait' message.
expected_result: API stays stable and prevents quota exhaustion of the Gemini API.

scenario: Scraped text contains Google Tag Manager or other tracking code
handling_logic: The verifyWithGemini function includes a 'cleanSources' filter that specifically removes strings containing tracking scripts before passing them to the AI.
expected_result: AI context is pure content, free from noisy tracking script noise.
